violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Last date of sale were lacking. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The flow pressure of the fresh hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) was not between 15-25 PSI as required. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Person in charge contacted maintenance to service the dishwasher at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC contacted maintenance to service the dishwasher at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
Rice was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

Multiple foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023

ROP fish was observed thawing with the package in tact. Vacuum sealed seafood must be thawed with package not in tact. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
